hypothesis: A gas-diffusion electrode engineered with a hierarchical dual-scale pore structure (nanopores/mesopores ≈50–200 nm within macropores ≈10–50 µm) and equivalent Ag loading will increase reactor yield by at least 30% relative to the spray-coated Ag GDEs used in this study under the paper's champion operating conditions by reducing in situ CO2 bubble size and improving i-CO2 mass transport, thereby simultaneously increasing JCO and UCO2.
